provenance.source_corpus: Regeneration dynamics of planted seedling-origin aspen (Populus tremuloides Michx.)

nodes.name: Cutting trees close to the soil surface

source_spans.text: Cutting trees close to the soil surface produced more root suckers than leaving a 25 cm stump.
Cutting trees close to the soil surface produced more root suckers than leaving a 25 cm stump.

nodes.name: Severing all lateral roots to a depth of 20 cm

source_spans.text: Severing all lateral roots to a depth of 20 cm from the main stem of the established aspen resulted in root sucker regeneration on the severed roots.
Severing all lateral roots to a depth of 20 cm from the main stem of the established aspen resulted in root sucker regeneration on the severed roots.
The smaller diameter trees (the SD stand) produced three times more stump sprouts than the larger diameter trees (the LD stand)
The smaller diameter trees (the SD stand) produced three times more stump sprouts than the larger diameter trees (the LD stand)
After clearfelling in 2016, the LD stand produced significantly more root suckers
